event: The heavenly bands descending from a sky of jasper in Paradise
symbols: prostrate figure on the ground — human humility before the divine vision
heavenly hosts descending on the mountain — the angelic procession from Paradise
mountain summit — the approach to the heavenly realm, the summit of Purgatory or vision of Paradise
brilliant light above — the divine radiance of God's presence
description: An engraving showing a lone nude figure lying prostrate on a hillside in the lower portion, gazing upward at a brilliant procession of luminous figures descending from the mountain peak above. The celestial beings appear as bright, shimmering forms arranged in ranks, their light contrasting with the dark, densely vegetated hillside below. Trees and shrubs fill the lower landscape. The sky at the summit is radiant with white light. The composition creates a powerful vertical movement from earth to heaven.
theological_description: Though this illustration accompanies Dante's Paradiso, it draws deeply from biblical imagery. The prostrate figure before the heavenly vision echoes Daniel's response to angelic visitation: 'I fell on my face in a deep sleep with my face to the ground' (DAN.10.9), and John's reaction in Revelation: 'When I saw him, I fell at his feet as though dead' (REV.1.17). The descending heavenly hosts recall Jacob's ladder with angels ascending and descending (GEN.28.12), and the angelic armies that Elisha's servant saw covering the mountain (2KGS.6.17). The mountain as the meeting place between heaven and earth is a fundamental biblical motif — Sinai, Zion, Tabor, and the Mount of Olives all serve as thresholds between the divine and human realms. The overwhelming light that crowns the peak echoes the Johannine declaration that 'God is light, and in him is no darkness at all' (1JN.1.5).

scripture_refs.reason: Jacob's ladder with angels ascending and descending — the heavenly host on the mountain
Falling prostrate before the divine vision, overwhelmed by heavenly glory
The mountain full of horses and chariots of fire — heavenly armies revealed to mortal eyes
God is light — the radiant summit from which the heavenly hosts descend

_meta.title: The heavenly bands Down from a sky of jasper lighted now In Paradise
